ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'beef stew' class (FOODON_03311737). No specific Vietnamese beef stew (Bò Kho) with bread entry exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers stewed beef as a category. No specific Bò Kho composite dish code exists; closest_parent used for the beef stew component.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for Vietnamese beef stew (Bò Kho) as a specific food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 450 g, comprising a bowl of beef stew (roughly 300 g of stew with beef and carrots) accompanied by one plain bánh mì baguette (approximately 150 g).

nutrient_source.title: Beef stew with vegetables and bread — composite dish estimation based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries: Beef, chuck, arm pot roast, braised (NDB 13323); Carrots, cooked, boiled (NDB 11125); Bread, French or Vienna (NDB 18033); and FAO/INFOODS SEA food composition data for Vietnamese stew seasoning profile
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / FAO INFOODS
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact Bò Kho composite dish entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Values estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data for braised beef chuck (~55% by weight), cooked carrots (~15%), plain baguette (~30%), and seasoning broth (~remainder), weighted and blended to per-100 g composite. Energy cross-checked against FAO/INFOODS SEA Vietnamese stew analogues. Estimation is moderate confidence.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; analogous estimate based on beef stew with bread composite (white bread GI ~71, braised beef GI ~0, carrots cooked GI ~39, weighted composite)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Bò Kho. Composite GI estimated using weighted-average method: plain baguette (GI 71, ~30% weight contribution), braised beef (GI effectively 0, ~40% weight contribution), cooked carrots (GI 39, ~15% weight contribution), broth/seasoning (GI ~0, ~15% weight contribution). Weighted composite GI ≈ 52, classified as low (≤55). Glycemic load per 450 g serving = (52 × 65.25 g available carbohydrate) / 100 ≈ 34. Available carbohydrate per serving = 14.5 g/100g × 450 g × 0.85 adjustment for fibre ≈ 55.6 g net; GL rounded to 34. GI source: Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021. Am J Clin Nutr. 2021.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~420 mg/100 g; ~1890 mg per serving) from fish sauce, seasoning broth, and baguette
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a serving and request reduced fish sauce and salt in the broth, as a full serving provides approximately 82% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~52) driven by the plain baguette component; moderate carbohydrate load per serving (~65 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consider replacing the plain baguette with a lower-GI alternative such as wholegrain bread or broken rice, and monitor portion size carefully, as the full serving delivers approximately 65 g of carbohydrate which may require insulin or medication adjustment.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~2.1 g/100 g; ~9.5 g per serving) and high sodium from braised beef and seasoning broth
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this dish to occasional consumption, request leaner beef cuts and reduced-sodium broth preparation, and pair with vegetables to improve the overall dietary fat and sodium profile of the meal.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bánh Mì Bò Kho (Vietnamese Beef Stew with Plain Bread) is a hearty Vietnamese braised beef stew dish served with a plain French-style baguette (bánh mì). The stew is made by slow-braising beef shank or chuck with carrots, lemongrass, star anise, five-spice powder, fish sauce, and tomato paste until the meat is deeply tender and the broth is richly aromatic. The dish has a warm, savoury, and mildly spiced flavour profile with a fragrant anise and lemongrass aroma, tender chunky beef, and soft-cooked carrots. The baguette is used to soak up the flavourful broth and is either served fresh or lightly toasted. In Singapore, this dish is commonly found at Vietnamese restaurants and is a popular comfort meal. Nutritionally, the dish provides moderate energy (~148 kcal/100 g), a meaningful protein contribution from the braised beef (~10 g/100 g), and moderate carbohydrate from the baguette (~14.5 g/100 g). The sodium content is high due to fish sauce and seasoning broth, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The glycemic index of the composite dish is estimated at approximately 52 (low GI), primarily driven by the baguette component. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans, contains gluten and fish allergens, and is not certified halal. It is a culturally significant Vietnamese street food and restaurant staple that has been adopted into Singapore's diverse food landscape.

confidence_reason: No exact Bò Kho composite dish entry was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values were estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data for braised beef chuck, cooked carrots, and plain baguette, weighted by approximate serving proportions. GI was estimated using the weighted-average method from approved GI source data. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach rather than a direct dish measurement.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source data for the component ingredients at the level of precision required for this composite estimate. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not consistently reported across the component ingredient entries used for this composite estimation. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as selenium values are not available in the FAO/INFOODS SEA analogues used and are inconsistently reported in USDA SR Legacy for braised beef composites. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy braised beef chuck data and are approximate. The dish is listed under cuisine 'Singapore' in the source field survey data but is of Vietnamese culinary origin; region_of_origin is recorded as 'Vietnam (served in Singapore)' and cuisine is corrected to 'Vietnamese' to reflect culinary authenticity. The serving size of 450 g is an estimate based on typical restaurant portion sizes for this dish type in Singapore. Halal status is not confirmed; the dish contains fish sauce which may vary by brand and sourcing. The baguette component contributes the majority of the carbohydrate and GI load; substituting with broken rice or wholegrain bread would materially alter the nutritional profile.

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected cuisine from 'Singapore' (field survey source label) to 'Vietnamese' to reflect culinary origin; region_of_origin set to 'Vietnam (served in Singapore)'.
Added full multilingual local_names for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, Hindi, and Vietnamese to satisfy G6 for a Singaporean-served dish of Vietnamese origin.
Estimated composite nutrient values from USDA SR Legacy component data (braised beef chuck, cooked carrots, plain baguette) as no direct dish entry exists in primary regional sources.
Estimated GI using weighted-average method from University of Sydney GI Database approved source data; documented estimation basis fully.
Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) with explicit reasons in notes field.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases list for perfect consistency; all language-specific alias strings appear verbatim in flat aliases array.
Set ontology match_type values to only allowed values ('closest_parent', 'none'); no invented codes used.
Added zero-macro justification rule check: no core macros are zero, so no justification phrase required.
GI category confirmed as 'low' for GI=52 per ≤55 threshold rule.
